# Melaya

> Melaya is an AI agentic orchestration platform with trading-grade discipline — and a flagship AI agentic trading product. Built by an operator, for operators. 1,370+ tools, 110+ specialized subagents, 20+ AI providers, secure connectors, per-workflow RAG with isolated vector stores, full audit trail, and a Rust-native engine with normalized market data across 70+ venues. One studio for building, running, and operating AI agents and multi-agent crews across any workflow — including autonomous trading crews — plus a unified REST + WebSocket API for multi-exchange market data and trading.

Melaya is a standalone agentic platform headquartered in the United Arab Emirates, solo-built by Antoine Roche, who spent the previous decade shipping production trading systems at BNP Paribas CIB (EQD eCommerce, 2018-2022), SingularityDAO (product manager, DeFi yield vaults and on-chain analytics, 2021-2025), and Singularity Venture Hub (head of trading operations, HFT market making and execution tooling, 2025-2026).

Design philosophy: agents should not be loose chatbots with unlimited powers. They need scoped tools, clear permissions, secure credentials, cost tracking, audit logs, data-quality monitoring, and human approval where risk is high. Melaya applies trading-grade discipline to a general automation platform.

## The three products

- Agentic Framework — the orchestration studio. Drag agents, tools, and models onto a canvas, wire them, and schedule/run/replay. 1,370+ scoped tools, 110+ subagents, 20+ model providers. Anything an LLM can reason about, an agent runs alongside your team.
- Trading Engine ("THE CORE") — the Rust-native core under every signal: normalized market data and order routing across 70+ venues with sub-microsecond dispatch on the hot path, exposed as one unified REST + WebSocket API.
- Agentic Trading Crew (flagship) — compose a seven-persona trading crew (Macro Analyst, TA Analyst, Quant Analyst, Sentiment Analyst, Risk Manager, Portfolio Manager, Execution Trader) you own. Each seat picks its model and data; Risk holds the veto; ten safety rails watch the chain; every order waits for your signature.

## Core capabilities

- 1,370+ agentic tools across web, code, data, ops, trading, communication, files, cloud, social, RAG, and MCP bridge.
- 110+ specialized subagents with prebuilt crews for research, analysis, execution, and review.
- Bring-your-own model across 20+ AI providers: Anthropic Claude, OpenAI, Google Gemini, NVIDIA, Mistral, DeepSeek, xAI Grok, Moonshot Kimi, Zhipu, Qwen, Cerebras, Groq, SambaNova, Upstage, Reka, MiniMax, and OpenRouter — plus local models via Ollama and LM Studio.
- Per-workflow RAG: isolated vector stores with hybrid BM25 + dense retrieval.
- Secure connectors: AES-256-GCM envelope encryption, managed-vault secrets, per-user credential isolation.
- Full observability: pipeline run logs, tool-call traces, model invocation cost and token tracking, error reasons.
- Human-in-the-loop: per-tool approval gates, approval queue, scoped permissions and roles.
- Multi-tenant: project-scoped RBAC, per-project credential vaults, isolated execution environments.

## AI agentic trading (flagship)

- Autonomous, multi-agent trading crews — specialized personas (Macro Analyst, TA Analyst, Risk Manager, Execution Trader, and more) running in sequence or parallel.
- Human-in-the-loop approval on every order: only the execution persona can place trades, and it pauses for operator approval; every decision is audited.
- Server-managed stop-loss / take-profit: the engine watches the live tape and fires exits when price crosses your level (spot and perpetuals).
- Cadence: time schedules, market-event triggers (e.g. price-drop or liquidation cascade), or hybrid (timer plus event preemption; sub-minute cadences use an event-mode cooldown floor).
- Safety rails: scoped permissions per persona, per-cycle write cap, per-tier daily order quota, daily LLM cost cap, position/exposure limits, drawdown and consecutive-loss circuit breakers, egress + tool allowlists, dry-run mode end to end, full audit trail.
- Reactive sidecars: four real-time watchers run beside the crew — Drawdown Sentinel and Macro Blackout (blockers that reject writes before approval) plus Funding Flip and Liquidation Cascade (triggers that wake the crew early).
- Paper or live: the same crew definition backs a simulated-broker paper launch or a connected-exchange live launch.
- Launchable via API: crews launch through POST /api/v1/strategies as strategyType "agent_crew" (paper or live), the persona pipeline + per-persona tools + cadence + safety riding in params. Order approvals are actioned in the Studio approval queue today; a programmatic approvals API and the broader agent-builder API (arbitrary non-trading agents, pipelines, RAG, connectors) arrive in v2. HITL is currently always-on; opt-out for fully-autonomous live crews is on the roadmap.

## Unified market & trading API

- One normalized REST + WebSocket interface over 70+ venues, powered by an in-house Rust engine.
- REST base: https://api.melaya.org — WebSocket base: wss://wss.melaya.org
- Market data: ticker, order book, OHLCV, public trades, exchange status/time, markets, currencies.
- Derivatives: funding rates, funding-rate history, open interest, open-interest history, instrument constraints.
- Streaming: live ticker, order book, OHLCV, public trades, and cross-exchange liquidations.
- Trading: account balances, orders, positions, fills, live order placement (create/cancel/amend/close, leverage & margin controls), agent-driven strategies, paper (sim-broker) trading, and native backtesting on the Rust engine. Reads, paper, and backtests need only the `mk_` key; live order placement also requires a connected exchange key.
- Authentication: API keys prefixed `mk_`, passed as `?apiKey=mk_...` or `Authorization: Bearer mk_...`.
- Official SDKs in 9 languages (TypeScript/JavaScript, Python, Go, Rust, Java, Kotlin, C#/.NET, Ruby, PHP) wrap the full surface with one identical API each.

## Links

- Website: https://melaya.org
- Documentation: https://melaya.org/docs
- Source & SDKs (GitHub): https://github.com/melaya-labs/melaya
- TypeScript/JavaScript SDK (npm): https://www.npmjs.com/package/@melaya/sdk
- Python SDK (PyPI): https://pypi.org/project/melaya/
- Rust SDK (crates.io): https://crates.io/crates/melaya
- Ruby SDK (RubyGems): https://rubygems.org/gems/melaya
- Go SDK (module): github.com/melaya-labs/melaya/packages/sdk-go/melaya
- Discord: https://discord.gg/2BBMUUdnkj
